title: Load Balancer Health Check Failure Removing Healthy Nodes
symptoms: Backend instances being removed and re-added to load balancer every 5-15 seconds
Intermittent 503 Service Unavailable errors during capacity dips
Health check logs showing rapid HEALTHY/UNHEALTHY state transitions
Auto-scaler launching excessive instances to compensate for perceived failures
Application response times normal when accessed directly
root_cause: Overly aggressive health check configuration with 1-second interval and single-check threshold causing false-positive failures from normal response time variance
resolution: Restored health check configuration to 10-second interval with 3 consecutive checks required, stabilized the backend pool, and terminated excess auto-scaled instances
preventive_actions: Implement health check configuration change validation with performance testing
Set minimum thresholds for health check intervals and healthy counts
Add backend pool stability monitoring for flapping detection
Create health check tuning guidelines documentation
Implement canary testing for load balancer configuration changes

timeline.event: Health check configuration change deployed to production
Backend instances begin flapping between healthy and unhealthy
503 error rate increases, auto-scaler begins launching instances
Alert fires for backend pool instability
Alex Johnson identifies health check change as trigger
Health check configuration reverted to previous settings
Backend pool stabilizes, excess instances terminated
Error rate at zero, incident resolved

embedding_summary: GCP Cloud Load Balancer health check misconfiguration with overly aggressive interval (1s) and threshold (1) caused healthy backends to flap in and out of serving pool. Intermittent 503 errors for 25 minutes affecting 3,800 users. Resolved by restoring conservative health check parameters.
